observations.polarity: House as physical structure vs. Heart as emotional/spiritual center
observations.summary: The four-room house in Hebron is metaphorically described as resembling a man's heart, divided against itself, highlighting a tension between the physical and emotional/spiritual realms.

left_evidence.quote: The four-room house in Hebron had become the shape of one man's heart

right_evidence.quote: a house would come to resemble something so treacherous as a human heart

bridge_evidence.quote: You might ask why a house would come to resemble something so treacherous as a human heart—and if you are asking that question, then you are paying the right kind of attention to what you are reading.

accepted_observations.summary: The four-room house in Hebron is metaphorically described as resembling a man's heart, divided against itself, highlighting a tension between the physical and emotional/spiritual realms.
